scenario: Vector Logistics, a freight broker calling 18000 rate-quote APIs per day, uses an outbound HTTP-JSON connector in Camunda 8.8 to fetch carrier prices. The team wants 12 retries spaced 30 seconds apart per call to absorb upstream throttling, replacing the default of 3 retries executed sequentially without any delay between attempts.
question: Which BPMN-level configuration delivers 12 retries spaced 30 seconds apart for this outbound connector?

options.text: Set retries="12" on the zeebe:taskDefinition element and add a retryBackoff task header bound to the ISO 8601 duration value PT30S in the element template
Set retries="12" on the zeebe:taskDefinition element and add a retryDelay task header bound to the ISO 8601 duration value PT30S in the element template
Set retries="12" in the element template properties and add a retryBackoff process variable that carries the ISO 8601 duration value PT30S at runtime
Set retries="12" on the zeebe:taskHeaders element and add a retryBackoff field on the task definition with the ISO 8601 duration value PT30S at deploy time

a.text: Correct. The retries attribute sits on the zeebe:taskDefinition element, and the connector runtime recognises retryBackoff as a reserved task header with an ISO 8601 duration value; binding retryBackoff to PT30S therefore spaces the 12 retries 30 seconds apart instead of running them immediately.

b.text: Incorrect. The reserved task header documented for spacing retries is retryBackoff, not retryDelay; an unknown header is ignored by the connector runtime, so the retries would still run back-to-back without any delay.

c.text: Incorrect. retryBackoff is a task header on the BPMN element template, not a process variable; the connector runtime reads the value from the task header at job-failure time, not from runtime process variables on the instance.

d.text: Incorrect. The retries attribute belongs to zeebe:taskDefinition (not zeebe:taskHeaders), and retryBackoff is a task header rather than a property on the task definition; the two schema slots have been swapped here.

explanation: Connector execution defaults to 3 immediate retries on failure. To change the count, set the retries attribute on zeebe:taskDefinition in the BPMN XML. To add a delay between attempts, bind a retryBackoff task header (a reserved key handled by the connector runtime) to an ISO 8601 duration such as PT30S; the property can be hidden or made visible by changing its type.
